name: Roscoe Mitchell
abstract: Roscoe Mitchell (born August 3, 1940) is an American composer, jazz instrumentalist, and educator, known for being "a technically superb – if idiosyncratic – saxophonist". The Penguin Guide to Jazz described him as "one of the key figures" in avant-garde jazz; All About Jazz stated in 2004 that he had been "at the forefront of modern mus…

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Roscoe Mitchell's views on free jazz as relayed by the interviewer, but provides no personal anecdote, collaboration, or direct interaction history.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening to ... Roscoe Mitchell ... I always loved that about Roscoe. ... I did have a write a paper and I wrote a paper on Roscoe.
